item.title: Tuesday's big stock stories: What’s likely to move the market in the next trading session
item.description: The S&P 500 made a comeback and closed marginally higher Monday as dip-buyers purchased shares amid the U.S.-Iran conflict.

item.title: Retail investors had two favorite trades during Monday's market turmoil
item.description: Mom-and-pop investors rushed into a pair of trades following the strikes on Iran.

item.title: Berkshire Hathaway shares drop nearly 5% after poor fourth-quarter results, no bold moves by new CEO Abel
item.description: Some had hoped for more aggressive signals on capital deployment given the company's swelling cash balance.

item.title: Dividend Aristocrats are outperforming this year in a volatile market. These stocks are Wall Street’s favorites
item.description: CNBC Pro screened for dividend aristocrats that are rated a buy by the majority of analysts covering the stock.

item.title: Energy set up for volatility near term but is turning the corner long term, says Katie Stockton
item.description: Long-term large-cap energy outlook remains bullish, supported by higher WTI crude oil. But the surge in BKR and XOM makes short-term charts look stret...
